calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Aug-28-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Kevin Urbatsch appeared via zoom for Aida Jones (petitioner).
Mercy Hall appeared via zoom.
Trevor Knoop (trust beneficiary) appeared via zoom.

Discussion was held in regard to Ms. Hall's fees, that there was no opposition from Mr. Urbatsch, and that Mr. Knoop is ready to have the conservatorship terminated. First account and report of trustee etc., is taken under submission.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR The Account For The Period Jan-18-22 To Jan-17-23
Off Calendar Apr-18-2023 Continued To Jun-20-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jessica Jones appeared by zoom for Aida Jones (trustee/petitioner).

The court noted that Mercy Hall was not present. At the court's inquiry, Mrs. Jones stated that the trust has been funded with just under $670,000.00, that they are working on finalizing the first accounting, that they should be able to file it soon and requested 60-days. Status on filing of the first accounting is continued to June 20, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The petition is to be filed by June 13, 2023.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Aeyoung Kim. Deputy: G. Tan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR The Account For The Period Jan-18-22 To Jan-17-23
Off Calendar Mar-14-2023 Continued To Apr-18-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Establish Special Needs Trust And Approve Attorney Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Feb-14-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mercy Hall, via ZOOM, for Laura Albert, via ZOOM, Petitioner; Donald David, via ZOOM, for Aida Jones, via ZOOM, proposed successor Trustee; Samuel Kornhauser, via ZOOM, for Laura Albert in related civil matter.

The court notes its interest in Ms. Jones' capacity to serve as successor trustee. Ms. Albert offers oral advocacy for Ms. Jones' appointment. The court, counsel, and Ms. Albert discuss the attorney fees being requested. Mr. David reports that he seems to remember an agreement wherein Ms. Hall's hourly rate would be $400 per hour but that the rate in the instant petition shows an hourly rate of $425. The parties and the court continue to discuss the disparity.

After additional discussion, the Petition to Establish SNT, etc., is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Records (Added For The Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar May-27-2021 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Records
Off Calendar Jun-01-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom: Mercy Hall and Samuel Kornhauser (via Courtcall) for and with Laura Albert. Donald David for Laura Albert and Trevor Knoop.

The Court stated that this is on for a motion to seal the record, that the Court issued a tentative ruling, that the moving party failed to establish an overriding interest in confidentiality and that there are no new arguments or facts. Mr. Kornhauser stated that he objected to the tentative ruling, argued that there is a need to enforce the resolution agreement and that confidentiality is a basic provision. The Court addressed the issues of 1. overriding interest in confidentiality, 2. no opposition is common in these motions, 3. parties in the civil matter agreeing to seal documents is not sufficient reason to seal documents, 4. parties understood the risk of the contractual penalties. Furthermore the Court stated that the parties knew that information would be disclosed in public proceedings, that notice and opportunity to be heard are much broader in Probate Court, questioned why these proceedings are not covered by the exception to the confidently agreement and why wouldn't a court order be sufficient. Mr. Kornhauser reported that he faces three litigious aggressive attorneys, that they claimed filing an in camera review was a breach, that they would face lawsuits claiming disclosure if the documents were not sealed and that those attorneys are doing everything they can to reduce the amount in the resolution. The Court stated that the parties entered into an agreement and that it cannot save them from that, that the Court cannot save them from aggressive attorneys, that parties knew there would be more proceedings before this case wrapped up and that there is not a single cases that gets a sealing order because everyone stipulates to it.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: Motion to seal records is taken under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Staff Attorney: Elif Sonmez-Kirsch. Clerk: N. Wong. Court reporter: Adreinne Meda. CSR:6609, telephone: 415-742-2361. Email Adrie.wcr@gmail.com. Bailiff: Deputy D. Johnson. Matter reported.
